skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar), Wan-AI Wan 2-7 (audio-driven mouth sync via `audio_url` on a portrait), HappyHorse 1.0 (Arena #1 t2v / i2v with in-pass audio), and Seedance v2 Pro (multi-modal cinematic with reference audio + reference subject). Picks the right model for the user's actual intent — UGC voiceover, virtual presenter, dubbed product demo, lip-synced character, dialog scene — and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke.
skill.summary: Routes user intent to the right audio-driven avatar model on RunComfy and ships documented prompts with minimal CLI invokes.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 115+ potential issues, but after contextual evaluation all findings are false positives. The 67 'external_commands' matches are markdown code blocks containing legitimate `runcomfy` CLI invocations, not actual shell execution. The 36 'network' matches are documentation URLs to runcomfy.com and runcomfy.net (the vendor's own catalog and docs). The 'hidden file in home directory' match refers to `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json`, the documented standard token storage path with mode 0600. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches land in plain documentation text, not crypto code. The 'system reconnaissance' matches are 'Avoid for' guidance text. The skill is well-documented with explicit security guidance on consent, shell injection boundaries, and outbound endpoint allowlists.

low_findings.title: External CLI command invocations
low_findings.description: The skill invokes the `runcomfy` CLI via Bash. The `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)` declaration in the skill frontmatter restricts agent execution to only runcomfy subcommands, preventing arbitrary command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of CLI invocations in code examples. These are legitimate runcomfy commands within a scoped allowed-tools boundary, not injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Outbound network requests to vendor API
low_findings.description: The skill sends data to model-api.runcomfy.net and related vendor endpoints as part of its core function. The skill documents an allowlist of only runcomfy.net/runcomfy.com domains with no telemetry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are documented as the core function (avatar video generation via vendor API). The allowlist and no-telemetry claim is stated in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Token file written to user home directory
low_findings.description: The `runcomfy login` command writes an API token to `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with mode 0600. This is standard CLI config behavior, not malware.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documented standard config path with file mode 0600 (owner read/write only). The skill itself describes this and recommends env var bypass for CI.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar and Lip-Sync Videos
content.value_statement: Creating avatar videos requires choosing the right model for audio-driven animation, lip-sync, and talking-head generation. This skill routes your intent to the correct RunComfy model and ships the exact CLI invoke.
actual_capabilities: Routes user intent to the best audio-driven avatar model (OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, HappyHorse, Seedance v2 Pro, Wan 2-2 Animate)
Generates talking-head videos from a portrait image and an audio file
Creates lip-synced videos with locked mouth motion to a specific audio track
Produces avatar videos from text scripts without an external audio file (HappyHorse in-pass audio)
Supports multi-reference cinematic compositions with Seedance v2 Pro (up to 9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio tracks)
Provides minimal `runcomfy run` CLI invokes with documented input schemas for each model
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account and authentication token for execution
Audio quality directly affects output quality for portrait+audio models
HappyHorse regenerates audio each call and cannot lock to a specific MP3
Requires explicit user-provided consent when using real people's likeness or voice

use_cases.title: UGC product ad with voiceover
use_cases.description: Create a vertical-format user-generated content ad using OmniHuman with a portrait and a voiceover MP3 in a single CLI call.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and social media content creators

use_cases.title: Multi-language brand video dubbing
use_cases.description: Use the same portrait image with different audio files per language to produce dubbed brand videos with consistent identity.
use_cases.target_user: Brand managers and international marketing teams

use_cases.title: Stylized character animation
use_cases.description: Animate illustrated characters, mascots, or anime-style art with full-body motion synchronized to audio using Wan 2-2 Animate.
use_cases.target_user: Game developers, animators, and creative agencies

prompt_templates.title: Basic portrait talking-head video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video from this portrait using OmniHuman: [IMAGE_URL] and this voiceover: [AUDIO_URL]. Use a clean voiceover without music bed for best lip-sync quality.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick avatar video from an existing portrait and audio file

prompt_templates.title: Script-to-video without audio file
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking-head video from this script using HappyHorse 1.0 t2v: "[SPOKEN_LINE]". Use a 9:16 aspect ratio at 1080p, 6 seconds duration. The subject should look at the camera and say the line clearly.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a written script but no audio recording

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic avatar with scene control
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wan 2-7 with audio_url to create this scene: [SCENE_PROMPT] with this voiceover: [AUDIO_URL]. The prompt describes the scene; the audio drives the mouth. Match the audio's emotional tone in the scene description.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full scene composition with locked lip-sync to specific audio

prompt_templates.title: Multi-reference cinematic monologue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Seedance v2 Pro with reference_images: [SUBJECT_URL], reference_audio: [VOICEOVER_URL]. Prompt: "[CINEMATIC_DESCRIPTION]". Duration 10s, aspect ratio 21:9. This composes subject, audio, and cinematic motion in one pass.
prompt_templates.scenario: High-end ad creative or cinematic avatar work with full creative control

output_examples.input: Create a UGC ad with this portrait [portrait.jpg] and this voiceover [voiceover.mp3]
output: Output: Single avatar video file in ./out, vertical format, subject speaks/sings/gestures to the audio

output_examples.input: Make this character [mascot.png] say this line: "Welcome to our app!" with a friendly tone
output: Output: Animated mascot video with mouth synchronized to generated speech

best_practices: Use clean voiceover audio without music beds for the best lip-sync quality
Choose head-and-shoulders or upper-body portrait framing for portrait-talks jobs
Keep HappyHorse scripts to 1-2 sentences per clip; chain clips for longer narratives
anti_patterns: Do not use HappyHorse when you need precise lip-sync to a specific MP3 (audio is regenerated each call)
Do not use Seedance v2 Pro for simple portrait+audio jobs (overpowered, slower, use OmniHuman instead)
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell; install the runcomfy CLI only via npm or npx

faq.question: Which model should I use for a simple portrait talking to audio?
faq.answer: Use OmniHuman (bytedance/omnihuman/api). It is the default for one-portrait-plus-one-audio jobs and produces strong single-shot results without needing a prompt.

faq.question: Can I generate a video from a script without an audio file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use HappyHorse 1.0 t2v and quote the spoken line inside the prompt with the phrase 'says clearly: "..."'. The model generates audio in-pass.

faq.question: How do I ensure good lip-sync quality?
faq.answer: Use clean voiceover audio without music beds. Isolate the voice stem if your audio is a mix. Match the audio's emotional tone in the scene description for Wan 2-7 and Seedance.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with real people's faces and voices?
faq.answer: The skill requires you to have rights to both the subject's likeness and the speaker's voice. It refuses requests targeting real people without consent and respects deepfake-disclosure norms.

faq.question: What does the runcomfy CLI do with my data?
faq.answer: The CLI posts input to the Model API at model-api.runcomfy.net, polls for status, and downloads results. There is no telemetry. The only outbound endpoints are runcomfy.net and runcomfy.com domains.

faq.question: How do I install the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Install via npm globally (npm i -g @runcomfy/cli) or use npx (npx -y @runcomfy/cli). Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell.
